ui.language.label: Language
ui.hero.title: Map public evidence. Ship a client-ready dossier.
ui.hero.body: OSINTPRO connects domain, repository, username and wallet signals into a defensible investigation graph with reports, exports and monitoring built in.
ui.finding.attacker: How an attacker may abuse it

security_header_missing.title: Missing security header
security_header_missing.description: Reduces the publicly observable browser-side security posture.
security_header_missing.attacker_abuse: An attacker would combine missing browser controls with another application bug, malicious embed or unsafe third-party content.
security_header_missing.business_impact: Customer sessions, forms, admin panels or embedded flows may be easier to abuse if another flaw exists.
security_header_missing.owner_action: Add the missing header with staged rollout and regression tests for critical pages.
security_header_missing.evidence_to_collect: Collect affected response paths, current header values, CSP/HSTS rollout status and iframe/script dependencies.

spf_missing.title: SPF missing
spf_missing.description: The domain does not publish an SPF policy in the main TXT records.
spf_missing.attacker_abuse: An attacker can send mail that appears aligned with the brand and rely on weak receiving checks.
spf_missing.business_impact: Invoice fraud, support impersonation, credential harvesting and brand-trust damage.
spf_missing.owner_action: Publish SPF for authorized senders, remove obsolete senders and pair it with DMARC reporting.
spf_missing.evidence_to_collect: Collect legitimate mail vendors, bounce domains, helpdesk workflows and spoofing complaints.

dmarc_missing.title: DMARC missing
dmarc_missing.description: No public anti-impersonation policy was found on _dmarc.
dmarc_missing.attacker_abuse: An attacker can imitate the domain while receivers lack a clear handling policy for failed authentication.
dmarc_missing.business_impact: Higher chance of executive impersonation, customer scams and vendor-payment fraud.
dmarc_missing.owner_action: Start with DMARC p=none plus reports, validate legitimate flows and move toward quarantine/reject.
dmarc_missing.evidence_to_collect: Collect aggregate reports, mail sources, third-party sender inventory and spoofing scenarios.

dmarc_policy_none.title: DMARC monitoring-only policy
dmarc_policy_none.description: DMARC is present, but p=none does not ask receivers to quarantine or reject failing mail.
dmarc_policy_none.attacker_abuse: Attackers may still benefit from spoofed messages because many receivers only report the failure.
dmarc_policy_none.business_impact: Brand abuse can continue while the organization believes DMARC has already solved spoofing risk.
dmarc_policy_none.owner_action: Use aggregate reports to fix legitimate senders, then move to quarantine and reject in stages.
dmarc_policy_none.evidence_to_collect: Collect rua reports, legitimate sender inventory, SPF/DKIM alignment state and exception owners.

dmarc_rua_missing.title: DMARC aggregate reports missing
dmarc_rua_missing.description: DMARC does not publish an observable rua reporting destination.
dmarc_rua_missing.attacker_abuse: Without aggregate reports, defenders lose visibility into spoofing attempts and legitimate alignment failures.
dmarc_rua_missing.business_impact: Slower enforcement rollout and weaker evidence for phishing-defense decisions.
dmarc_rua_missing.owner_action: Add a monitored rua mailbox or reporting processor before stronger enforcement.
dmarc_rua_missing.evidence_to_collect: Collect reporting mailbox ownership, privacy review and expected report processor.

dmarc_ruf_missing.title: DMARC forensic reports not configured
dmarc_ruf_missing.description: No ruf destination was observed for forensic DMARC failure reporting.
dmarc_ruf_missing.attacker_abuse: Defenders may have less sample-level evidence when investigating targeted spoofing campaigns.
dmarc_ruf_missing.business_impact: Lower incident triage quality during executive impersonation or vendor fraud attempts.
dmarc_ruf_missing.owner_action: Decide whether forensic reports are appropriate for the jurisdiction and privacy model.
dmarc_ruf_missing.evidence_to_collect: Collect legal/privacy position, reporting mailbox owner and incident response use case.

tls_rpt_missing.title: TLS-RPT missing
tls_rpt_missing.description: No SMTP TLS reporting policy was observed at _smtp._tls.
tls_rpt_missing.attacker_abuse: Mail delivery TLS failures may go unnoticed, reducing evidence during downgrade or delivery issues.
tls_rpt_missing.business_impact: Harder troubleshooting of secure mail delivery and lower visibility into misconfiguration.
tls_rpt_missing.owner_action: Publish TLS-RPT with a monitored reporting address after validating mail routing ownership.
tls_rpt_missing.evidence_to_collect: Collect mail provider, reporting mailbox and expected report handling process.

tls_expiring_soon.title: TLS expiring soon
tls_expiring_soon.description: The TLS certificate is close to expiry.
tls_expiring_soon.attacker_abuse: Attackers do not need to break TLS: outage windows can push users toward insecure workarounds or fake support links.
tls_expiring_soon.business_impact: Service outage, failed checkout/login flows and increased phishing success during confusion.
tls_expiring_soon.owner_action: Verify automated renewal, alert owners and rehearse renewal failure recovery.
tls_expiring_soon.evidence_to_collect: Collect certificate owner, renewal automation logs, alert routing and dependency inventory.

security_txt_missing.title: security.txt missing
security_txt_missing.description: No standard public security disclosure channel was found.
security_txt_missing.attacker_abuse: A researcher or customer who finds a weakness may struggle to report it, increasing disclosure risk.
security_txt_missing.business_impact: Slower vulnerability intake, missed reports and reputational damage from unmanaged disclosure.
security_txt_missing.owner_action: Publish security.txt with contact, policy and expiry fields routed to the right owner.
security_txt_missing.evidence_to_collect: Collect current security contact, intake SLA, disclosure policy and escalation owner.

caa_missing.title: CAA missing
caa_missing.description: The domain does not publicly restrict which CAs can issue certificates.
caa_missing.attacker_abuse: If a certificate authority or validation process is abused, there is no DNS-level policy limiting issuance.
caa_missing.business_impact: Weaker certificate governance and harder incident scoping after suspicious issuance.
caa_missing.owner_action: Add CAA records for approved CAs and document who can change certificate policy.
caa_missing.evidence_to_collect: Collect current CAs, wildcard usage, ACME providers and certificate issuance history.

dnssec_not_observed.title: DNSSEC not observed
dnssec_not_observed.description: No public DS/DNSKEY records were found from the local resolver.
dnssec_not_observed.attacker_abuse: In some DNS attack or misrouting scenarios, unsigned zones provide less cryptographic assurance.
dnssec_not_observed.business_impact: Lower DNS integrity assurance for mail, web and certificate validation records.
dnssec_not_observed.owner_action: Evaluate DNSSEC support with the registrar and DNS provider before enabling.
dnssec_not_observed.evidence_to_collect: Collect registrar support, DNS provider support, rollback plan and operational ownership.

asn_provider_unknown.title: Hosting provider attribution incomplete
asn_provider_unknown.description: Public IP RDAP context did not clearly identify every observed address owner.
asn_provider_unknown.attacker_abuse: During incident response, unclear hosting ownership slows takedown, abuse desk contact and asset accountability.
asn_provider_unknown.business_impact: Longer triage windows for shadow infrastructure, forgotten deployments or third-party routing issues.
asn_provider_unknown.owner_action: Map each public IP to a hosting owner, account owner and abuse contact in the asset register.
asn_provider_unknown.evidence_to_collect: Collect IP RDAP data, cloud account owner, contract owner and current deployment purpose.

subdomain_takeover.title: CNAME to managed providers
subdomain_takeover.description: Some subdomains point to SaaS/cloud platforms: verify ownership to prevent takeover.
subdomain_takeover.attacker_abuse: An attacker may look for abandoned cloud/SaaS resources referenced by DNS and try to claim the unowned resource.
subdomain_takeover.business_impact: Brand impersonation under a trusted subdomain, phishing pages, cookie exposure or customer-data capture depending on host use.
subdomain_takeover.owner_action: Verify every managed-provider CNAME is claimed by an active account and remove stale DNS records.
subdomain_takeover.evidence_to_collect: Collect DNS owner, cloud/SaaS account owner, last deployment date and proof of resource ownership.

wallet_dust_pattern.title: Dust transaction pattern
wallet_dust_pattern.description: Several very small-value transactions appear in the recent window.
wallet_dust_pattern.attacker_abuse: An investigator should review whether the wallet is being tagged, spammed or linked through dusting activity.
wallet_dust_pattern.business_impact: Potential privacy loss, noisy attribution or extra review work during fraud reconstruction.
wallet_dust_pattern.owner_action: Preserve the transaction IDs and avoid assuming attribution until timing and counterparties are reviewed.
wallet_dust_pattern.evidence_to_collect: Collect transaction hashes, timestamps, counterparties and public explorer notes.

wallet_mixer_proximity.title: Proximity to tagged mixer/privacy counterparty
wallet_mixer_proximity.description: Recent counterparties include public labels compatible with mixer or privacy services.
wallet_mixer_proximity.attacker_abuse: This can indicate proximity to obfuscation infrastructure, but it is not proof of control or intent.
wallet_mixer_proximity.business_impact: Compliance, recovery and fraud teams may need extra hop analysis and documentation.
wallet_mixer_proximity.owner_action: Expand one hop at a time, record tag source and separate proximity from attribution.
wallet_mixer_proximity.evidence_to_collect: Collect tag source, transaction IDs, hop count and exchange/mixer label evidence.

wallet_exchange_proximity.title: Proximity to tagged exchange/service counterparty
wallet_exchange_proximity.description: Recent counterparties include public labels compatible with centralized services or exchanges.
wallet_exchange_proximity.attacker_abuse: Funds may have moved through a service where preservation or legal requests are relevant.
wallet_exchange_proximity.business_impact: Recovery or compliance workflow may depend on timely service-provider contact.
wallet_exchange_proximity.owner_action: Record service labels, transaction IDs and escalation path before making claims.
wallet_exchange_proximity.evidence_to_collect: Collect public labels, transaction hashes, timestamps and case notes.

report.methodology_body: OSINTPRO uses passive public DNS, HTTPS, certificate and well-known resources. It does not execute exploit payloads, brute force credentials or modify the target.
report.methodology_email_note: Missing email controls are findings only when mail service or an email policy is observed. Public robots.txt and sitemap.xml files are expected indexing metadata, not vulnerabilities by themselves.
report.score_note: Score reflects material risk only. Low-severity notes below do not affect scoring.
report.publication_not_vulnerability: Publication is not a vulnerability by itself.
report.findings_disclaimer: Findings are passive observations and review hypotheses. Validate impact in an authorized environment before presenting them as confirmed vulnerabilities.
report.no_priority_passive_detail: The observed public surface did not produce a high-priority hypothesis.
report.no_attacker_path: No attacker path was inferred from passive evidence.
report.no_material_impact: No material business impact was identified from this passive snapshot.
report.keep_monitoring: Keep monitoring DNS, TLS and browser security headers for drift.
report.baseline_evidence: Baseline DNS, TLS and header state for future drift checks.
report.email_scope_unknown: Email scope could not be determined.
report.indexing_contextual: Public indexing files are contextual metadata, not vulnerabilities by default.
report.methodology_pdf_body: OSINTPRO collected passive public signals from DNS, HTTPS responses, certificate metadata, Certificate Transparency and public well-known resources. It did not execute exploit payloads, brute force credentials, scan private address ranges or attempt to modify the target.
report.rule_email_controls: Missing email controls are findings only when mail service or an email policy is observed.
report.rule_robots_sitemap: robots.txt and sitemap.xml are expected public metadata and are not vulnerabilities by themselves.
report.rule_optional_wellknown: OpenID, mobile association files and BIMI are optional unless the product claims those capabilities.
report.rule_cname_takeover: CNAME takeover signals require ownership validation; a managed-provider CNAME alone is not proof of exploitability.
report.rule_ct: Certificate Transparency is an asset-discovery and monitoring source, not a vulnerability.
report.passive_boundary_body: This report is evidence for defensive review, not proof of identity, compromise or exploitability. Findings should be confirmed by the asset owner or an authorized security professional.

phrases.summary_missing_headers: Domain is reachable. Missing {count} observable security headers.
phrases.recommendation_missing_headers: Add or review missing security headers: {headers}.

static.Domain is reachable, but the HTTPS heade: Domain is reachable, but the HTTPS header assessment was unavailable.
static.Domain is reachable, but the public emai: Domain is reachable, but the public email posture needs attention.
static.Domain is reachable with the main securi: Domain is reachable with the main security headers present.
static.Mail service or email authentication rec: Mail service or email authentication records were observed.
static.Header not found in the HTTPS response: Header not found in the HTTPS response
static.Content-Security-Policy was not observed: Content-Security-Policy was not observed on the main HTTPS response.
static.Validate with authorized application tes: Validate with authorized application testing and define a CSP for scripts, frames and connect-src.
static.Deploy a report-only CSP first, then enf: Deploy a report-only CSP first, then enforce.
static.New MX, NS, CAA or TXT records can indic: New MX, NS, CAA or TXT records can indicate infrastructure changes.
static.Correlate observed technology with harde: Correlate observed technology with hardening and patch policy.
static.Use OSINTPRO only on assets, brands, pub: Use OSINTPRO only on assets, brands, public profiles and wallets you own, manage, investigate with permission or review for legitimate public-interest defense.
static.Pick one evidence source, then move ever: Pick one evidence source, then move everything into cases, graphs, monitoring and exports.
static.Domain, social, wallet and repository si: Domain, social, wallet and repository signals.
static.Web, repo, network and game security wor: Web, repo, network and game security workflows.
static.Reports, graph exports, alerts and webho: Reports, graph exports, alerts and webhooks.
static.Reports, monitors and graph nodes stay s: Reports, monitors and graph nodes stay scoped to the active account and client folder.
static.This does not prove the repository is vu: This does not prove the repository is vulnerability-free. Continue with dependency, architecture and runtime testing.
static.Passive sources do not show major issues: Passive sources do not show major issues.
static.No vulnerability hypotheses emerged from: No vulnerability hypotheses emerged from passive sources.
static.Impact depends on reachability, privileg: Impact depends on reachability, privilege and exposed data.
static.Confirm applicability, assign an owner a: Confirm applicability, assign an owner and track remediation.
static.Collect logs, ownership context and curr: Collect logs, ownership context and current configuration.
static.The domain does not resolve IP addresses: The domain does not resolve IP addresses from the local backend.
static.Verify the TLS certificate: OSINTPRO did: Verify the TLS certificate: OSINTPRO did not read a valid HTTPS expiry date.
static.Establish a baseline and monitor DNS, TL: Establish a baseline and monitor DNS, TLS and header drift.
static.Public surface is limited in passive sou: Public surface is limited in passive sources.
static.New certificates can reveal subdomains,: New certificates can reveal subdomains, shadow IT or unexpected issuance.
static.CSP, HSTS, frame and MIME headers often: CSP, HSTS, frame and MIME headers often regress during application deployments.
static.CNAMEs to external providers should be m: CNAMEs to external providers should be mapped to owners and contracts to prevent takeover.
Email scope could not be determined.
static.Scope: write down the exact domain you a: Scope: write down the exact domain you are allowed to test.
static.Proxy: inspect one normal browser reques: Proxy: inspect one normal browser request before changing anything.
static.Repeater: resend only safe GET requests: Repeater: resend only safe GET requests such as the homepage or public metadata files.
static.Evidence: save headers, status codes and: Evidence: save headers, status codes and screenshots for the client report.
static.prioritize missing headers, disclosure f: prioritize missing headers, disclosure files, TLS expiry and email spoofing posture.
static.Review the prioritized issues below befo: Review the prioritized issues below before touching any tooling.
static.Use this lab only on domains, apps and a: Use this lab only on domains, apps and accounts you own or are explicitly authorized to test. OSINTPRO does not automate exploitation, brute force, credential attacks, bypasses or payload delivery.
static.write down the exact domain you are allo: write down the exact domain you are allowed to test.
static.inspect one normal browser request befor: inspect one normal browser request before changing anything.
static.resend only safe GET requests such as th: resend only safe GET requests such as the homepage or public metadata files.
static.save headers, status codes and screensho: save headers, status codes and screenshots for the client report.
static.Main browser security headers are presen: Main browser security headers are present.
static.Use this lab only on domains, apps and a: Use this lab only on domains, apps and accounts you own or are explicitly authorized to test. OSINTPRO provides passive evidence, beginner education and documentation workflows. Misuse against third-party systems is prohibited and remains the responsibility of the operator.
